medical_history.chief_complaint: Epigastric pain followed by hypovolemic shock due to intraperitoneal hemorrhage
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Admitted to Tours Hospital in October 1991 for epigastric pain followed by hypovolemic shock due to intraperitoneal hemorrhage. Emergency laparotomy showed 5500 mL of blood coming from a large rupture of Glisson’s capsule. The liver was enlarged and soft, and Pringle’s maneuver temporarily stopped bleeding. Macroscopic examination and frozen sections of wedge biopsy specimens made in the right and left lobes and extemporaneously examined showed no evidence of tumor. Selective hepatic arteriography showed unusual distal vascularization. Bleeding was definitively stopped in a second laparotomy by ligation of the hepatic artery and gauze packing. After depacking and cholecystectomy in a third laparotomy, the outcome was marked by jaundice and acute pancreatitis; however, the patient was discharged 1 month later in good condition.
medical_history.family_history: The father of case A1 had type 2 diabetes mellitus at the age of 60 years and died at the age of 73 years without clinical evidence of liver disease.

physical_examination.general_examination: Hypovolemic shock due to intraperitoneal hemorrhage. The liver was enlarged and soft.

laboratory_examinations.findings: Normal liver function test results, plasma α-fetoprotein concentration, and fasting glucose level
Heterozygous R229X germline mutation identified in HNF-1α

radiographic_examinations.findings: Hypodense nodules (>10) and a 12-cm pediculated liver mass of segment III that became hyperdense after injection
Numerous nodules (>10) consistent with liver adenomatosis
Typical aspect of liver adenomatosis with numerous nodules (>10)

other_tests.findings: Typical aspects of hepatocellular adenoma with steatosis and no signs of hepatocellular dysplasia
Numerous foci of microvesicular and macrovesicular steatosis, intermingled with clear hepatocytes, irregularly dispersed in the parenchyma
Heterozygous 164–168del, G55fsX57 germline HNF-1α mutation identified

diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Familial liver adenomatosis associated with hepatocyte nuclear factor 1α inactivation
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on the presence of multiple adenomas within an otherwise normal hepatic parenchyma, confirmed by imaging (MRI and CT scans) and histologic examination. Genetic testing identified germline HNF-1α mutations in affected family members, supporting the diagnosis. The reasoning also included the exclusion of other potential causes through clinical history, physical examination, and blood analysis.

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: Emergency procedure to stop intraperitoneal hemorrhage

treatment_information.specific_treatment: ligation of the hepatic artery and gauze packing
treatment_information.dosage_or_details: Second laparotomy to definitively stop bleeding
